description: You are a structural bioinformatician studying hemoglobin variants. Your task is to extract the individual protein chains from the canonical human deoxyhemoglobin crystal structure (~/UGENE_Data/hemoglobin_4HHB.pdb), align them, and summarize the differences between the alpha and beta subunits. Export the amino acid sequence of each chain (A, B, C, and D) as separate FASTA files in the ~/UGENE_Data/results/ directory named exactly chain_A.fasta, chain_B.fasta, chain_C.fasta, and chain_D.fasta. Create a combined multi-FASTA file containing all four chain sequences at ~/UGENE_Data/results/all_chains.fasta. Perform a multiple sequence alignment of these four chains using the MUSCLE algorithm and export the resulting alignment in ClustalW format to ~/UGENE_Data/results/chains_alignment.aln. Finally, write a summary report to ~/UGENE_Data/results/chain_analysis_report.txt that includes the total number of chains extracted, an identification of which chains are alpha-type and which are beta-type, and an observation on whether the alpha chains are identical to each other and how they differ from the beta chains based on your alignment.
